message:
feat(service): implement actual timeout logic for TimeoutFallback cancellation mode

The TimeoutFallback mode was previously a no-op placeholder that simply awaited
the future directly without any timeout enforcement. This change implements
real timeout behavior:

- When fallback_timeout is configured and a timer driver is available, uses
  the crate's timeout helper to enforce the deadline
- Returns TowerAdapterError::Timeout when the deadline is exceeded
- Falls back to best-effort (no timeout) when no timer driver available
- Preserves backward compatibility when fallback_timeout is None

Also adds comprehensive test coverage for the timeout behavior:
- Uses VirtualClock and TimerDriverHandle for deterministic testing
- Creates a PendingService that never resolves to trigger timeout
- Verifies that advancing time past the deadline produces Timeout error
- Tests the full poll cycle: Pending -> advance clock -> Ready(Err(Timeout))

This completes the implementation that was marked as a placeholder in the
original code comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
